explanation: The requirement is that for every DM_i, whenever the Local Node receives a HELLO message from DM_i, the message must be forwarded to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set and MPR Set, which then trigger their respective update and recalculation actions. The most direct LTL encoding is to use a response pattern: globally, if 'hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node' occurs, then eventually ('F') we must have (msg_forwarded_to_two_hop_neighbor_set & update_performed_by_two_hop_neighbor_set & msg_forwarded_to_mpr_set & recalculation_performed_by_mpr_set). These are ANDed together because all are required. This fits template 94, using the bounded response time (within 10 steps).

This LTL property states that whenever a HELLO message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node, it is always the case that the message will eventually be forwarded to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set and the MPR Set, and that this triggers an update in the 2 Hop Neighbor Set and a recalculation in the MPR Set, respectively. The 'for every DM_i' part is captured by universal (global) quantification (G), and the immediate reaction to the message being forwarded is modeled using the globally operator implying immediate (next) action. Template 28 G (bool_exp1 -> G[0,] bool_exp3) is used, where bool_exp1 represents the sending of the HELLO message, and bool_exp3 represents the requirement that both the message is forwarded to the needed components and the appropriate updates/calculations occur.

This property expresses that whenever a HELLO message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), then eventually (at some point thereafter) all of the following must eventually occur: the message is forwarded to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set (msg_forwarded_to_two_hop_neighbor_set), an update occurs in the 2 Hop Neighbor Set (update_performed_by_two_hop_neighbor_set), the message is forwarded to the MPR Set (msg_forwarded_to_mpr_set), and a recalculation is performed by the MPR Set (recalculation_performed_by_mpr_set). This is expressed with a U (until) operator in the LTL template, meaning that after the HELLO message, the considered forwardings and updates must eventually be observed for each DM_i. The template chosen applies the property for every occurrence of the HELLO message from DM_i to the Local Node, as required.

The input property requires that whenever a HELLO message from DM_i arrives at the Local Node, two things must eventually happen: (1) the message is forwarded to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set component, which then updates its information, and (2) the message is forwarded to the MPR Set component, which then initiates a recalculation. Since each sequence must happen as a response to the HELLO message, we can capture this with the 'if-then-globally-never-not' template (template 28), where the Boolean consequence must be a boolean combination, not temporal. Thus, we express as a boolean conjunction: '((msg_forwarded_to_two_hop_neighbor_set -> update_performed_by_two_hop_neighbor_set) & (msg_forwarded_to_mpr_set -> recalculation_performed_by_mpr_set))'. This means that it is always the case that, after the HELLO message is received, if the message is forwarded to the respective set, then the corresponding update/recalculation must occur.

This property captures that whenever a HELLO message has been sent from DM_i to the Local Node, it is always the case that the message is forwarded to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set component (triggering an update) and to the MPR Set component (triggering a recalculation). Since the property is stated to hold for every DM_i, we use a global 'always' operator and model the required actions as a temporal response to the initial event. This is achieved via a response property with nested 'eventually' operators to guarantee that after the HELLO message is sent to the Local Node, forwarding to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set (with update) and MPR Set (with recalculation) will eventually happen. Template 28 suits this requirement: G (bool_exp1 -> G[0,] bool_exp3). We set bool_exp1 as 'hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node' and bool_exp3 as '(msg_forwarded_to_two_hop_neighbor_set & update_performed_by_two_hop_neighbor_set) & (msg_forwarded_to_mpr_set & recalculation_performed_by_mpr_set)'. All other boolean expressions and timing parameters are left empty/null as not required by the template.

The property states: For every execution (for every DM_i), whenever a HELLO message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), it must eventually be forwarded to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set (msg_forwarded_to_two_hop_neighbor_set) and then cause an update (update_performed_by_two_hop_neighbor_set), and also be forwarded to the MPR Set (msg_forwarded_to_mpr_set), which then triggers a recalculation (recalculation_performed_by_mpr_set). The LTL formula uses an implication over G (globally), capturing 'for every DM_i', and applies a chain of U (until) expressing that after the message is sent, all required actions must occur. This matches the intention that these actions must always happen after every such input message.

This LTL property specifies that whenever a HELLO message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), eventually the message is forwarded to 2 Hop Neighbor Set (msg_forwarded_to_two_hop_neighbor_set) and to MPR Set (msg_forwarded_to_mpr_set), followed (eventually) by their respective side effects: update_performed_by_two_hop_neighbor_set and recalculation_performed_by_mpr_set. We encode this by requiring that after the trigger (bool_exp1), these events (bool_exp3) all happen, i.e., are 'eventually' satisfied (U). The template G (a -> (b U c)) is encoded using template 93, with bool_exp4 describing the overall required effect after the trigger, here grouped with AND. We leave any unused bool_exp4 as 'True' to avoid syntax errors, or substitute as needed, but in this case template 93 does not require bool_exp4. Consequently, the required postcondition is specified as a conjunction in bool_exp3. This precisely matches the requirement stated in the natural language input for every DM_i, and no extraneous conditions are included.

The property states: Whenever a HELLO message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node, it must eventually be forwarded to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set (for update) and to the MPR Set (for recalculation) for every DM_i. This is captured by a global implication: if 'hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node' happens, globally it is required that the message is forwarded to both these components, with the corresponding actions happening eventually.

We use the template: G (bool_exp1 -> G[0,] bool_exp3), with bool_exp1 expressing when the HELLO arrives at the Local Node (from DM_i), and bool_exp3 encoding the 'must forward to 2 Hop Neighbor Set and MPR Set and trigger their associated actions' (a conjunction of both forwarding and the resulting actions). 'Globally' is used because it must hold for every DM_i.

N_DURATION1 is set, for completeness, to 1 (but actually, the inner G[0,] means 'everywhere after', i.e., always, after the implication triggers).

This LTL formula encodes the property that whenever a HELLO message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node, the Local Node must forward this message to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set component (which should trigger an update there) and also forward the message to the MPR Set component (which should trigger a recalculation). Since the property should hold globally (for all DM_i, and at all times), we use a template expressing that if the HELLO message is sent, then it is always the case that it will (globally) cause the required forwardings and result in the required corresponding updates. This is best captured with template 28, using a conjunction for bool_exp3. Note: We do not need to express temporal order (like 'after'), as the property asserts only that if the message is sent, then both forwardings/updates happen without specifying strict causality or ordering between them, and holding globally ("for every DM_i").

This LTL property expresses: 'For every time a HELLO message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (hello_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), it is always the case that, globally, after that, the message is forwarded to the 2 Hop Neighbor Set (msg_forwarded_to_two_hop_neighbor_set) and to the MPR Set (msg_forwarded_to_mpr_set); and that an update is performed by the 2 Hop Neighbor Set component (update_performed_by_two_hop_neighbor_set), and a recalculation is performed by the MPR Set (recalculation_performed_by_mpr_set)'.
